content: Elliott Wave Theory posits markets move in 5-wave impulses followed by 3-wave corrections. Waves reflect crowd psychology. Impulse waves (1,3,5) go with trend; corrective waves (2,4,A,B,C) against. Wave 3 typically strongest. Rules: Wave 2 never retraces >100% of Wave 1; Wave 3 never shortest. Criticized for subjectivity: different analysts count waves differently. Works best as framework, not precise prediction tool.
